subject: Product Management
title: MySpace vs Facebook
description: A fast breakdown of MySpace vs Facebook for builders.

hook.text: MySpace was the biggest social network in the world in 2006. Three years later, it was a cautionary tale.

buildup.text: MySpace had strong PMF with teens who loved customizing profiles with glitter, music, and chaotic HTML.

discovery.text: Facebook offered the opposite: clean design, real identities, and a college-exclusive feel that created aspirational value.

twist.text: MySpace doubled down on customization and advertising while Facebook invested in the social graph and developer platform.

climax.text: Facebook didn't just win users — it changed what social networking meant. MySpace's version of PMF became obsolete.

punchline.text: Your fit can be disrupted by a better definition of the category.

quiz.question: How did Facebook displace MySpace?
options: By redefining what social networking meant — clean design, real identity, developer platform
